author: y4rr
content:
Pass 5.B.5 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOW-UPS.

Pattern B (device-resident migration) applied to all 3 transforms:
- Normalize::apply: from_vec(...).to(input.device()) for mean/std + sub + div
- RandomHorizontalFlip::apply: index_select_dim with reverse i64 indices
- RandomCrop::apply: narrow x2 + .contiguous()

All 3 now device-resident through their entire chain. All 4 discriminating tests added (preserves device + numerical correctness). 162/162 lib tests, build + clippy -D warnings clean.

CUDA dependency: RandomHorizontalFlip routes through index_select_dim, which lacks a CUDA forward kernel (already tracked as #1098, Pass 3 follow-up). On CUDA inputs it now returns Err(NotImplementedOnCuda) instead of silently demoting — correct PyTorch-faithful surface per the no-silent-fallback north star.

Subagent honest catches: (1) architect's sabotage probe was tautological on Linux (CPU==CPU passes either way); subagent added a stronger numerical sabotage (drop-the-divide) that hard-panics on Linux. (2) An optional 5th test was structurally unreachable (no_grad-wrapped apply body) and was honestly omitted with documented reason.
